title: Ethereum Foundation Turns AI Loose on ETH Network to Find Bugs Before Hackers Do
summary: Ethereum researchers are using AI agents to hunt for vulnerabilities, shifting security work from finding bugs to proving which ones are real.
body:
The Ethereum Foundation is turning to artificial intelligence to bolster the security of the Ethereum network. Researchers are using AI agents to proactively hunt for vulnerabilities, shifting the focus from merely finding bugs to proving which ones are real and exploitable.

This initiative is part of a broader effort to stay ahead of potential hackers. By leveraging AI, the foundation hopes to identify and address security issues before they can be exploited, thereby protecting the network and its users. The AI agents are designed to analyze the codebase and simulate various attack scenarios to uncover potential weaknesses.

For everyday users, this means a more secure Ethereum network. As the blockchain ecosystem continues to grow, the risk of exploits and hacks increases. By using AI to preemptively find and fix bugs, the Ethereum Foundation is taking a proactive approach to safeguarding user assets and maintaining trust in the network.

The next step for users is to stay informed about any updates or patches released by the Ethereum Foundation. Keeping your software up to date and following security best practices will help ensure that you are protected against potential vulnerabilities.
